ddt:Target.Name: FrontCameraADAS_D2C_SW1p0_v3
ddt:Target.Description:
15/12/2021 : FrontCameraADAS_D2C_SW1p0_v1 - Anas MOUHIBILLAH Création de la base DDT FrCAM ADAS SWEET400.
20/12/2021 : FrontCameraADAS_D2C_SW1p0_v2 - Manon DAVID Modification of DiagOnCanFD to match the T3_2021 Message Set on which SW1.0 was make (7DC --> 7C3 and 7DD --> 7C9)
22/12/2021 : FrontCameraADAS_D2C_SW1p0_v3 - Manon DAVID Modification of Soft and Version of Auto-identification Add of Project Application xJBph2 and x1316

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: OutputControlCommand
Data.Comment: Input Output control parameter list

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Label.Text: Write Camera and vehicle parameters according to your needs
Init screen and press stop button
Now, perform CODING => See Coding screen
Set "ECU not configured" first and "ECU configured" then. This transition unblocks coding, otherwise NACK22 - execution conditions not set !!!
After Reset, read EyeQ_Status until it changes from unknow to DV_mode or Vision_Mode => OK. The DTC configuration should be not present anymore now
Check coding type is triggered by routine. If not, change it
Set ECU not configured and ECU not caibrated
Coding must be done AFTER CONFIGURATION. see Config screen
Init screen and press stop button
1: if never configured or parameters changed
Leave all zeros. simply launch the routine

Button.Text: Request Aiming Routine Results - Normal Mode

Label.Text: 1: if never configured or parameters changed
yaw rate and vehicle speed read under test

Initialise the screen with the green button,then click on programming session button, then click on the locker icon to unlock the ECU, check on the log that ECU is unlocked, and then click on the button writeFDXX
